name: proser
displayName: Proser — Make VSCode the ultimate writing app
description: Creative writing with local AI editor

untrustedWorkspaces.description: AI network features (Revise with AI, context-aware synonyms) are disabled in untrusted workspaces.

commands.title: Set Word Goal for This Document
Move Proser Panel to the Right (Secondary) Side Bar

viewsWelcome.contents:
Open a project folder to see your manuscript chapters.
[Open Project Folder](command:workbench.action.files.openFolder)

No chapters here yet. Choose the folder that holds your manuscript chapters.
[Set Story Folder](command:proser.storyMemory.chooseFolder)

proser.thesaurus.source.description: Where to fetch synonyms/antonyms: online (Datamuse), offline (bundled WordNet), or auto (Datamuse with offline fallback).

proser.thesaurus.maxResults.description: Maximum number of synonym/antonym suggestions to show.

enumDescriptions: Ask the first time you look up a word.
Use the AI model (context-aware) first, falling back to the dictionary.
Use the local dictionary (Datamuse/WordNet) only.
proser.thesaurus.aiMode.description: Source for synonyms/antonyms. Switch anytime from the editor right-click menu.

enumDescriptions: Free Dictionary API — richest entries (phonetics, examples, meanings by part of speech). Requires internet.
Bundled WordNet (offline) — ~155k words, no network needed.
Free Dictionary API first, with offline WordNet fallback when offline or the word isn't found.
proser.dictionary.source.description: Where the right-click Dictionary looks up a word's meaning.

proser.spellcheck.enabled.description: Enable spell checking in Markdown files.

proser.spellcheck.debounceMs.description: Delay before re-checking spelling after edits, in milliseconds.

enumDescriptions: No space after a sentence-ending period.
One space after a sentence-ending period.
Two spaces after a sentence-ending period.
proser.spacing.afterPeriod.description: Expected number of spaces after a sentence-ending period. Sentences whose spacing differs get a yellow underline in the editor (logic-based, no AI). Line and paragraph breaks are never flagged.

enumDescriptions: American: periods & commas go inside the closing quote (…past.”). Flags the British placement.
British: periods & commas go outside the closing quote (…past”.). Flags the American placement — best-effort; may over-flag full-sentence dialogue.
Don’t check punctuation placement around quotation marks.
proser.quotes.punctuationStyle.description: Where periods and commas sit relative to a closing double quotation mark. The non-preferred placement gets a teal underline in the editor (logic-based, no AI). Only double quotes are checked.

proser.checks.passiveVoice.description: Underline passive-voice sentences live in the editor (purple, logic-based — instant, no AI).

proser.checks.tense.markdownDescription: Underline tense-inconsistent sentences live in the editor (orange). Uses the local AI model (independent of the Spell Check toggle); it's throttled, cached, and skipped on very large files (use the sidebar's **Scan** for those). Needs a capable model (Gemma-class or larger).

proser.checks.grammar.description: Underline grammar / word-choice errors live in the editor (blue, AI proofread). Turn this off to silence the blue squiggles while keeping AI spell-checking on.

enumDescriptions: English (built in)
Spanish — Español (downloads on first use)
French — Français (downloads on first use)
German — Deutsch (downloads on first use)
Italian — Italiano (downloads on first use)
Portuguese — Português (downloads on first use)
Dutch — Nederlands (downloads on first use)
Russian — Русский (downloads on first use)
proser.spellcheck.language.markdownDescription: Language to spell-check in. English is built in; other languages download their dictionary once (a few MB) and then work offline. Switch quickly with the **Proser: Select Spell Check Language** command. Korean/CJK aren't supported offline (the engine can't process their dictionaries).

proser.qualityLint.enabled.description: Flag weasel words, passive voice, and filler words in Markdown files.

proser.wordcount.statusBarEnabled.description: Show the word count, reading time, and goal progress in the status bar for Markdown files.

proser.wordcount.includeCodeBlocks.description: Count words inside fenced and inline code.

proser.wordcount.wordsPerMinute.description: Reading speed used to estimate reading time.

proser.wysiwyg.syncDebounceMs.description: Delay before syncing external file changes into the pretty editor, in milliseconds.

proser.wysiwyg.fontSize.description: Font size (px) for the Proser pretty editable view. The default 18 approximates an average book's body text at comfortable screen-reading distance.

proser.wysiwyg.maxWidth.markdownDescription: Width of the text column in the pretty view, centered in the pane (no border or page frame). Prefer `ch` units (characters per line) so the measure stays book-like as you zoom — the default `80ch` is a generous reading measure, and ~60–75 is the print 'average book' sweet spot. You may also use `px` (e.g. `720px`), physical units `mm`/`cm`/`in`, or `%`. Use `none` for full width.

proser.manuscript.exclude.markdownDescription: Extra file basenames to skip when compiling a folder into a manuscript (matched case-insensitively against the filename without `.md`). Common reference files (readme, notes, bible, threads, arcs, review, changelog, todo, memory) are always skipped.

proser.ai.engine.description: AI backend for 'Revise with AI' and context-aware synonyms: off, OpenRouter (cloud), or Ollama (local).

proser.ai.openrouter.model.description: OpenRouter model slug used for AI features.

proser.ai.openrouter.preferGroq.description: Prefer the Groq provider on OpenRouter (with fallback) for faster inference.

proser.ai.ollama.model.description: Ollama model tag used for local AI features.

proser.ai.ollama.endpoint.description: Base URL of the local Ollama server.

proser.ai.ollama.brainstormContextTokens.markdownDescription: Max context window (tokens) for **Brainstorm** and the **Story Memory** it injects. The folded canon is budgeted to ~70% of this (so the 50000 default fits ~35k of memory), and it never overflows. **Bigger fits more of your story memory but uses more VRAM** (the model's KV cache grows with it). Capped by the model's own context window and a 200000 ceiling. Raise it to fit a larger memory if you have the VRAM; lower it if the model runs out of memory.

proser.ai.spellAi.markdownDescription: Use your AI model for **spelling** (clears dictionary false-positives like names/coined words/sounds and grades grammar). Turn off for Hunspell **dictionary only**. Set it from **Proser Settings → Spell Check**. (Synonyms use **Settings → Synonyms**: AI / Online / Offline.)
